name: Msue- Macomb County (Clinton Township)
description_short: MSU Extension's MI Money Health provides free, unbiased financial and homeownership education through certified counselors, webinars, and self-paced learning resources to Michigan residents.
description_long:
MI Money Health is part of Michigan State University Extension, a university-based educational program designed to serve all Michigan residents regardless of financial status. The program grew out of MSU's commitment to expanding access to personal finance and homeownership education across diverse communities throughout the state. Rather than operating as a traditional for-profit financial services company, MI Money Health functions as an educational resource hub operated by a university extension system with certified housing and money management counselors on staff.

The organization offers multiple formats of financial education including online and in-person classes, foreclosure prevention assistance, homeownership seminars, money management instruction, and self-paced learning materials. They provide specialized programs such as Rent Smart (covering financial preparation and tenant responsibilities), estate planning guidance ("Who Gets Grandma's Yellow Pie Plate?"), and youth-focused programs like Smart Money Missions. Consumers can access articles, videos, publications, and events, or contact certified financial or housing experts directly. Their current event schedule includes homeownership seminars, mortgage foreclosure webinars, and interactive financial literacy games for teens.

MI Money Health is distinguished by its university affiliation, certified counselor credentials, and commitment to unbiased, accessible education with no apparent commercial agenda. The program publishes impact reports documenting outcomes from their financial and homeownership education initiatives. Their approach emphasizes demystifying financial concepts and best practices rather than selling specific products or services. The organization serves a broad demographic and explicitly states its goal is ensuring every Michigan resident has educational opportunity regardless of financial status.

The primary limitation is that MI Money Health is education-focused rather than a direct service provider—they teach financial concepts but do not offer credit repair, loans, debt settlement, or other transactional financial products. The organization is limited to Michigan residents, and some services require attendance at specific in-person locations or webinar times. While their credential-based approach is a strength, consumers seeking immediate financial solutions (emergency loans, credit fixes) should look elsewhere.
pros: Certified housing and money management counselors deliver trusted education with university credentials
Free access to financial education resources with no apparent sales agenda or commercial motivation
Multiple delivery formats including in-person classes, virtual webinars, self-paced articles/videos, and direct expert consultation
Specialized programs addressing specific needs: foreclosure prevention, first-time homebuying, rental preparation, and youth financial literacy
Published impact reports (2020, 2021) documenting measurable outcomes from their financial education programs
Serves diverse audiences regardless of financial status with explicit equity commitment
Covers comprehensive topics from basic money management to complex areas like estate planning and mortgage options
cons: Education-only provider with no ability to directly resolve financial problems like bad credit, missed payments, or debt obligations
Limited to Michigan residents only; no services for consumers in other states
In-person programs require attendance at specific locations and times, creating scheduling barriers for some consumers
Website navigation includes broken link (404 error on outreach/about page), suggesting potential resource management challenges
No evening or weekend event options visible in current calendar, limiting access for working-hour-constrained populations
best_for: Michigan residents preparing to buy their first home and needing foundational homeownership education
Homeowners facing foreclosure seeking prevention guidance and mortgage options counseling
Renters and prospective tenants needing financial preparation and tenant responsibility education
Young adults (ages 13-19) seeking youth-focused financial literacy through interactive programs
diagnosis: MI Money Health is best for Michigan residents seeking free, credential-backed financial education and homeownership guidance delivered without commercial sales pressure. The primary caveat is that they provide education and counseling only—they cannot directly resolve credit problems, provide loans, negotiate debts, or conduct credit repair; consumers needing transactional financial services should consult other providers.
services: Free online homeownership education webinars (MSHDA program)
Mortgage foreclosure options counseling (webinars covering keep/sell/default choices)
Money management education and financial literacy instruction
Self-paced online learning resources (articles, videos, publications)
Estate planning family discussion facilitation ("Who Gets Grandma's Yellow Pie Plate?")
Youth financial literacy program - Smart Money Missions Goosechase game (ages 13-19)
Custom community presentations on emerging financial topics
Financial and homeownership program impact reports and research
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 21885 Dunham Rd., Suite 12, Verkuilen Bldg., Clinton Twp, MI 48036
